info.title: SMB Sales Boost API
info.description: REST API for SMB Sales Boost - a B2B lead generation platform providing access to newly registered small and medium businesses across the United States. Search, filter, and export leads with comprehensive company data including contact information, location, and AI-enriched categories. API access requires a Starter, Growth, Scale, Platinum, or Enterprise subscription plan. New users can purchase a subscription entirely via API using the Programmatic Purchase endpoints (no web signup required). A Model Context Protocol (MCP) server is also available at https://smbsalesboost.com/mcp for AI assistant integration - see https://smbsalesboost.com/docs/api/mcp-server for details.

post.description: Create a Stripe Checkout session for purchasing a subscription entirely via API. No web signup or onboarding required. Returns a checkout URL and a claim token. After payment is completed at the checkout URL, use POST /api/v1/claim-key with the claim token to retrieve your API key. Rate limited to 5 requests per hour per IP.

checkoutUrl.description: Stripe Checkout URL - redirect or open this to complete payment

claimToken.description: Claim token (claim_ prefix + 32 random bytes). Use with POST /api/v1/claim-key after payment.

expiresAt.description: When this purchase session expires (24 hours from creation)

409.description: An active subscription already exists for this email

post.summary: Claim your API key after payment
post.description: After completing payment at the Stripe Checkout URL, use this endpoint with your email and claim token to retrieve your API key. The API key is returned once and cannot be retrieved again. If payment is still pending, returns status 'pending' - poll every 5-10 seconds. Rate limited to 30 requests per hour per IP.

apiKey.description: Your API key (smbk_ prefix). Store securely - shown only once.

202.description: Payment not yet completed - poll again in 5-10 seconds

403.description: Email does not match the purchase record

409.description: API key has already been claimed

get.description: Retrieve your user profile including subscription details, account settings, and onboarding status.

patch.description: Update your profile fields. Only provided fields are updated. Allowed fields: firstName, lastName, companyName, companyWebsite.

get.description:
Search the lead database with keyword filters, location filters, and other criteria. Requires at least one positive filter (positiveKeywords or column-specific include terms). Uses a two-phase filtering model: Phase 1 (Include) combines all positive keywords with OR logic, Phase 2 (Exclude) removes matches via AND logic.

Credit System: Each new lead returned costs 1 credit. Previously-exported leads are free to re-query. Use maxCredits and maxResults parameters to control credit usage. When either is specified, credit-optimized ordering is applied: new leads (credit-consuming) are sorted first, then previously-exported leads fill remaining slots.

Two Database Types: The 'home_improvement' database contains contractor and home service businesses with phone numbers as the available contact method. The 'other' database contains all other business types (e.g., accounting firms, marketing agencies, medical practices) with both phone numbers and email addresses as available contact methods.

Wildcard Pattern Matching: All keyword and term parameters support `*` as a wildcard character that matches any sequence of characters. For example, `*account*` matches 'accounting', 'accountant', 'accounting firm', etc. Multiple wildcards in a single term let you match compound phrases: `*book*keep*` matches 'bookkeeping', 'bookkeeper services', etc. Other examples: `*market*agency*`, `*tax*prep*`. Keywords without wildcards perform substring matching by default (equivalent to wrapping in `*...*`).

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Page number

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Results per page (max 1000)

parameters.name: database
parameters.description: Database type. 'home_improvement' contains contractors/home service businesses (phone numbers only). 'other' contains all other business types (phone numbers and email addresses). Defaults to your account setting.

parameters.name: positiveKeywords
parameters.description: JSON array of keywords to include. Matched via OR across default columns. Supports * wildcard for flexible pattern matching (e.g., ["*account*", "*book*keep*", "*tax*prep*"]). Keywords without wildcards perform substring matching by default.

parameters.name: negativeKeywords
parameters.description: JSON array of keywords to exclude. Checked against same columns as positive keywords. Supports * wildcard (e.g., ["*franchise*"]).

parameters.name: orColumns
parameters.description: JSON array of column names for keyword matching. Overrides defaults for both positive and negative keywords.

parameters.name: stateInclude
parameters.description: Comma-separated US state codes to include (e.g. CA,NY,TX)

parameters.name: stateExclude
parameters.description: Comma-separated US state codes to exclude

parameters.name: cityInclude
parameters.description: JSON array of cities to include

parameters.name: cityExclude
parameters.description: JSON array of cities to exclude

parameters.name: zipInclude
parameters.description: JSON array of ZIP codes to include

parameters.name: zipExclude
parameters.description: JSON array of ZIP codes to exclude

parameters.name: nameIncludeTerms
parameters.description: JSON array of business name include terms

parameters.name: nameExcludeTerms
parameters.description: JSON array of business name exclude terms

parameters.name: redirectFilter
parameters.description: Filter by redirect status (other only)

parameters.name: updateReasonFilter
parameters.description: Comma-separated update reasons to filter by (e.g., Newly Added,Phone Primary)

parameters.name: websiteSchemaFilter
parameters.description: Comma-separated website schema types to filter by (other only, e.g., LocalBusiness,Organization). Use GET /leads/other/schema-types for available values.

parameters.name: lastUpdatedFrom
parameters.description: Filter leads by Last Updated date (after this date). For Home Improvement leads, Last Updated means a new phone number was detected. For Other leads, it means any of the 5 contact/address fields changed: primary phone, secondary phone, primary email, secondary email, or full address. Also includes newly added records. This is the primary way to identify the freshest leads - many businesses launch a website before adding contact info, so Last Updated captures when that information first becomes available.

parameters.name: lastUpdatedTo
parameters.description: Filter leads by Last Updated date (before this date).

parameters.name: timeScrapedFrom
parameters.description: Filter leads scraped after this date (ISO 8601 or relative format e.g. rel:30d)

parameters.name: timeScrapedTo
parameters.description: Filter leads scraped before this date (ISO 8601)

parameters.name: registrationDateFrom
parameters.description: Filter by domain registration date start (other only). Accepts ISO or relative format (e.g. rel:6m)

parameters.name: registrationDateTo
parameters.description: Filter by domain registration date end (other only, ISO 8601)

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Full-text search across all fields

parameters.name: countryInclude
parameters.description: JSON array of countries to include

parameters.name: countryExclude
parameters.description: JSON array of countries to exclude

parameters.name: maxResults
parameters.description: Maximum total leads to return (new + previously-exported). New leads are prioritized first, then previously-exported leads fill remaining slots. When specified, credit-optimized ordering is applied.

parameters.name: maxCredits
parameters.description: Maximum number of credits to spend on this query. Only new (credit-consuming) leads count toward this cap. Previously-exported leads are included for free up to the maxResults or page limit. Set to 0 to only receive previously-exported leads at no credit cost. If omitted, no credit cap is applied (standard credit deduction).

creditsUsed.description: Number of credits consumed by this query (only present for credit-plan users)

creditsRemaining.description: Total credits remaining after this query (only present for credit-plan users)

maxResults.description: Echo of maxResults parameter (only present when maxResults was specified)

maxCredits.description: Echo of maxCredits parameter (only present when maxCredits was specified)

402.description: Insufficient credits. Use maxCredits or maxResults to limit credit usage, reduce page size with limit parameter, or purchase more credits via POST /purchase-credits.

get.summary: Preview leads with masked contact info (no credits)
get.description: Preview leads matching your filters with masked/hashed contact information. Supports the same filters as GET /leads (keywords, location, company name, URLs, descriptions) except contact-field filters (email/phone include/exclude are not available on preview). Does NOT consume any credits. Use this to test your filters and evaluate result quality before committing credits on the full GET /leads endpoint. Contact fields (phone numbers and email addresses) are returned in a masked format (e.g., '(5**) ***-**89', 'j***@***.com'). Response includes preview: true flag.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Results per page (max 1000)

parameters.name: database

parameters.description: JSON array of keywords to include. Supports * wildcard.

parameters.name: negativeKeywords

200.description: Preview leads with masked contact information

leads.description: Leads with masked contact fields (phone numbers and emails are hashed/masked)

get.description: Returns a sorted list of all distinct website schema types found in the Other leads database. Use these values with the websiteSchemaFilter parameter on GET /leads.

post.description: Export leads matching your filters as downloadable files. Supports CSV, JSON, and XLSX formats. Up to 10,000 leads per export. For credit-plan users (Starter, Growth, Scale, Platinum, Enterprise), each net-new lead exported deducts 1 credit. Rate limited to 1 export per 5 minutes.

filters.description: Same filter parameters as GET /leads (positiveKeywords, negativeKeywords, stateInclude, etc.)

selectedIds.description: Specific lead IDs to export (alternative to filters)

formatId.description: Export format template ID. Uses default format if not specified.

maxLeads.description: Maximum number of leads to include in this export. Overflow leads are stored in the lead reservoir for the next export. If omitted, all matching leads are exported.

maxResults.description: Maximum total leads to return (both new and previously-exported). New leads (credit-consuming) are prioritized first, then previously-exported leads fill remaining slots. If omitted, all matching leads are included.

maxCredits.description: Maximum number of credits to spend on this export. Only new (credit-consuming) leads count toward this cap. Previously-exported leads are included for free up to the maxResults limit. Set to 0 to only receive previously-exported leads. If omitted, no credit cap is applied (standard credit deduction).

creditsUsed.description: Number of credits deducted for net-new leads (credit-plan users only)

creditsRemaining.description: Remaining credit balance after this export (credit-plan users only)

maxLeads.description: Echo of maxLeads parameter (only present when maxLeads was specified)

overflowCount.description: Number of leads stored in the reservoir due to maxLeads cap (only present when maxLeads is specified)

maxResults.description: Echo of maxResults parameter (only present when maxResults was specified)

maxCredits.description: Echo of maxCredits parameter (only present when maxCredits was specified)

402.description: Insufficient credits to complete this export (credit-plan users only)

get.description: Retrieve all saved filter presets for your account.

post.description: Save a new reusable filter configuration.
Create a new keyword list with include and exclude keywords.

get.description: Retrieve all configured email delivery schedules.

post.description: Set up automated email delivery of leads matching a filter preset. Requires an active subscription.

patch.description: Update schedule settings. Only provided fields are updated.

get.description: Retrieve all custom export format templates.

post.description: Create a custom export format template with column mapping and file settings.
Set an export format as your default for all exports.

get.description: View past exports with details including date, row count, and filters used.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of entries to return

get.description: Re-download a previously exported file. Exports expire after 7 days.
View your current database type and whether you can switch.

daysRemaining.description: Days until switching is allowed again

post.description: Switch between 'home_improvement' and 'other' databases. Incompatible email schedules will be automatically paused.
Get AI-powered SMB category suggestions based on your company profile. Optionally analyzes your company website for better results. Rate limited to 5 requests per minute.

companyDescription.example: We provide accounting software and bookkeeping services to small businesses

productService.description: Specific product or service you sell
productService.example: Cloud accounting software, payroll services, tax preparation

companyWebsite.description: Your company website URL (optional, used for AI analysis)

post.description: Trigger AI-powered keyword generation based on your company profile and target categories (up to 3 per list). Keywords are generated as wildcard patterns and saved to keyword lists with auto-refine enabled by default. Runs asynchronously - use /ai/keyword-status to check progress. Rate limited to 5 requests per minute.

Purchase permanent credits using your saved payment method (Stripe off-session charge). Provide either creditCount (minimum 100) or dollarAmount (minimum $1). Maximum per purchase is 5x your plan's monthly allocation (Starter: 2,500, Growth: 10,000, Scale: 50,000, Platinum: 500,000, Enterprise: 1,250,000). When using dollarAmount, credits are calculated from your plan's overage rate and you are charged only for whole credits. Any fractional cents are rounded down in your favor. Pricing: Starter 10¢, Growth 7.5¢, Scale 5¢, Platinum 3¢, Enterprise 2¢ per credit.

creditCount.description: Number of credits to purchase (min 100, max 5x your plan's monthly credits). Mutually exclusive with dollarAmount.

dollarAmount.description: Dollar amount to spend on credits (minimum $1). Credits are calculated from your plan rate and you are charged for whole credits only. Mutually exclusive with creditCount.

402.description: Payment failed. Card was declined or requires authentication. Update your payment method via the dashboard and retry.

403.description: Not on an active credit plan

get.description: Get your auto top-up configuration including trigger threshold, purchase amount, monthly spending cap, and current month's usage.

creditsAutoTopUp.description: Credits purchased via auto top-up this month

amountAutoTopUp.description: Dollar amount spent on auto top-up this month (e.g. '$12.50')

amountAutoTopUpCents.description: Amount spent on auto top-up this month in cents

capResetAt.description: When the monthly usage cap resets

pricePerCredit.description: Price per credit for your plan (e.g. '$0.10')

pricePerCreditCents.description: Price per credit in cents (e.g. 10, 7.5, 5, 3, or 2)

403.description: Not on an active credit plan

patch.description: Configure automatic credit purchases. When enabled, credits are purchased automatically when your permanent credit balance falls below the trigger threshold. Supports trigger by credits or dollars, purchase by credits or dollars, and an optional monthly spending cap.

triggerAmount.description: Threshold value that triggers a purchase

purchaseType.description: Whether to buy by credit count or dollar amount

capType.description: Monthly cap type, or null to disable

creditsAutoTopUp.description: Credits purchased via auto top-up this month

amountAutoTopUp.description: Dollar amount spent on auto top-up this month (e.g. '$12.50')

amountAutoTopUpCents.description: Amount spent on auto top-up this month in cents

capResetAt.description: When the monthly usage cap resets

pricePerCredit.description: Price per credit for your plan (e.g. '$0.10')

pricePerCreditCents.description: Price per credit in cents (e.g. 10, 7.5, 5, 3, or 2)

403.description: Not on an active credit plan

post.description: Cancel your subscription at the end of the current billing period. Your access continues until the period ends.

Upgrade or downgrade your subscription plan. Available target plans: starter, growth, scale. On upgrade, unused monthly credits are automatically converted to permanent credits (capped at current plan's standard allocation). Card on file is charged the prorated difference for upgrades; downgrades take effect at renewal.

get.description: Retrieve all leads/domains on your export blacklist.

post.description: Add a lead or domain to your export blacklist. Supports single entries or batch adding via the 'entries' array.

oneOf.description: Single entry added (when using single value/entryType request)
Array of entries added (when using batch entries request)

get.description: Get auto-refine status for keyword lists. Optionally filter by a specific list ID. Returns refinement progress, quality scores per round, timing, and paired list information. The auto-refine system uses a single-pass 4-phase process: Phase 1 validates each positive keyword individually (50% threshold, up to 2 variation attempts), Phase 1B discovers up to 15 new positives (5-added target), Phase 2 checks negative keyword collateral damage (40% threshold), Phase 2B discovers up to 5 new negatives. High-volume keywords use multi-sample scoring (3x50 leads, median). Final quality score (1-10) determines if retry pass needed.

parameters.name: listId
parameters.description: Filter by specific keyword list ID

oneOf.description: Array of all keyword lists with refine info (when no listId specified)
Single list refine info (when listId specified)

404.description: Keyword list not found (when listId specified)

post.description: Enable AI auto-refine for a keyword list and start the single-pass 4-phase optimization process. Phase 1 validates each positive keyword individually (50% applicability threshold) with up to 2 variation attempts (3 candidates each). Phase 1B discovers up to 15 new positive keywords in a single AI call, testing all concurrently (5-added target based on incremental lead contribution). Phase 2 checks each negative keyword for collateral damage (40% threshold) with the same variation strategy. Phase 2B samples ~80 leads and discovers up to 5 new negative keywords. High-volume keywords use multi-sample scoring (3x50 leads, median). Final quality score (1-10, median of 3) determines if retry needed (score <=5). Auto-refine turns off when complete. Each list uses its sourceCategories (max 3) for accurate AI scoring. Rate limited to 5 requests per minute.

listId.description: Keyword list ID to enable auto-refine for

post.description: Disable AI auto-refine for a keyword list. Pauses any in-progress refinement and stops future optimization runs. Rate limited to 60 requests per minute.

listId.description: Keyword list ID to disable auto-refine for

get.description: Check the status of keyword generation jobs. Rate limited to 60 requests per minute.

post.description: Manually trigger an active email schedule to send immediately. Rate limited to 1 request per 5 minutes.

BearerAuth.description: API key with 'smbk_' prefix. Generate from Dashboard > API tab. Available on Starter, Growth, Scale, Platinum, and Enterprise plans.

currentRound.description: Current refinement round (0 if not started)

scoresPerRound.description: Quality scores from each completed round. Uses multi-sample scoring (3×50 leads, median) for high-volume keywords.

reasoning.description: Explanation of the score, e.g. 'Median of [6, 7, 5]'

lastRefinedAt.description: ISO timestamp of last refinement run

pairedListId.description: ID of the paired positive/negative keyword list (refined together)

totalLeadsExported.description: Total leads exported across all time

monthlyLeadsExported.description: Leads exported in the current billing cycle

monthlyCredits.description: Total monthly credit allocation for the plan

monthlyCreditsRemaining.description: Monthly credits remaining this billing cycle

permanentCredits.description: Purchased permanent credits (never expire, used after monthly credits)

creditOverageRate.description: Cost per additional credit in cents (e.g., 10 = $0.10 for Starter, 7.5 = $0.075 for Growth)

autoTopUp.description: Auto top-up configuration for automatic credit purchases

Lead.description: Lead data. Fields vary by database type and use display-name keys (with spaces). See HomeImprovementLead and OtherLead for the exact fields returned by each database type.

HomeImprovementLead.description: Lead from the Home Improvement database (Yelp-sourced). Phone numbers are the primary contact info. Field keys use display names with spaces.

Last Updated.description: Date the lead record was last updated. A new phone number was detected, or the record was newly added. Primary way to identify fresh, actionable leads.

OtherLead.description: Lead from the Other database (web registration-sourced). Provides both phone numbers and email addresses. Field keys use display names with spaces.

Last Updated.description: Date the lead record was last updated. Any of the 5 contact/address fields changed (primary phone, secondary phone, primary email, secondary email, or full address), or the record was newly added.

Phone Primary.description: Primary phone number (masked for free users)

Phone Secondary.description: Secondary phone number (masked for free users)

Email Primary.description: Primary email address (masked for free users)

Email Secondary.description: Secondary email address (masked for free users)

type.description: Whether this list contains include or exclude keywords

sourceCategories.description: Target categories this list was generated for (max 3 per list for optimal AI quality)

autoRefineEnabled.description: Whether AI auto-refine is active for this list

pairedListId.description: ID of the paired positive/negative list (auto-refine manages both together)

sourceCategories.description: Target categories (max 3 per list for best keyword quality)

pauseReason.description: Why the schedule is paused. 'user' = manually paused, 'insufficient_credits' = auto-paused due to zero credits, null = not paused (active)

splitParts.description: Which file split parts this recipient receives (only relevant when export format uses split files)

recipients.description: Recipients as objects with email and optional splitParts assignment

distributionMode.description: How leads are distributed among recipients

fullCopyRecipients.description: Recipients who receive the full lead list when distributionMode is split_evenly

maxLeadsPerEmail.description: Maximum leads per email delivery. Overflow stored in lead reservoir. Null = no cap.

combinedFileEnabled.description: Whether combined file delivery is enabled

combinedRecipients.description: Recipients who receive the merged combined file

combinedIncludeAssignee.description: Whether to add assignee attribution column to merged file

combinedIncludeFileName.description: Whether to add file name attribution column to merged file

combinedColumnPosition.description: Where to place attribution columns in merged file

combinedAssigneeColumnName.description: Custom header for the assignee column (1-50 chars)

combinedFileNameColumnName.description: Custom header for the file name column (1-50 chars)

filterPresetId.description: ID of the filter preset to use

splitParts.description: Which file split parts this recipient receives (only relevant when export format uses split files)

recipients.description: Recipients as objects with email and optional splitParts assignment

distributionMode.description: full_copy sends all leads to every recipient; split_evenly divides leads evenly among recipients

fullCopyRecipients.description: Optional recipients who receive the full lead list when distributionMode is split_evenly (e.g. managers)

maxLeadsPerEmail.description: Maximum leads per email delivery. Overflow stored in lead reservoir. Null = no cap.

splitParts.description: Which file split parts this recipient receives

distributionMode.description: full_copy sends all leads to every recipient; split_evenly divides leads evenly among recipients

fullCopyRecipients.description: Recipients who receive the full lead list when distributionMode is split_evenly

maxLeadsPerEmail.description: Maximum leads per email delivery. Overflow stored in lead reservoir. Null = no cap.

databaseType.description: Which database this format is designed for

combinedAssigneeColumnName.description: Default header for assignee column in combined files (1-50 chars)

combinedFileNameColumnName.description: Default header for file name column in combined files (1-50 chars)

databaseType.description: Which database this format is designed for

combinedAssigneeColumnName.description: Default header for assignee column in combined files (1-50 chars)

combinedFileNameColumnName.description: Default header for file name column in combined files (1-50 chars)
